helpful: Full petition text with the lower-court orders as appendices made the vehicle/split analysis possible without any docket fetches.
Statpack relist-count and per-circuit cuts gave a concrete anchor for a zero-relist, waived-response petition.
gaps: statpack has no salience-band table (the prompt references one) and no fee-class (paid vs IFP) grant-rate cut in the markdown; had to reason about the paid-petition adjustment qualitatively.
`fedcourts query --era modern` silently returns zero rows; the era vocabulary (2020s, 1990s, ...) is only discoverable from the statpack.
No easy corpus cut for 'respondent waived response / call-for-response' status, which is one of the strongest deny-side signals at the petition stage.
notes: Forward cell; ~4 retrieval calls total, well under budget.
